# Books by Stuart Russell

[Stuart Russell](https://people.eecs.berkeley.edu/~russell/) is a British computer scientist. He is a professor of computer science as well as engineering at UC Berkeley, and an honorary fellow of Wadham College, Oxford.

[](https://fivebooks.com/book/artificial-intelligence-modern-approach-russell-norvig/buy)

## [Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach](https://fivebooks.com/book/artificial-intelligence-modern-approach-russell-norvig/)

#### by [Peter Norvig](https://fivebooks.com/people/peter-norvig/) & [Stuart Russell](https://fivebooks.com/people/stuart-russell/)

_Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach_ is by Peter Norvig, director of research at Google and Stuart Russell, Professor of Computer Science and Smith-Zadeh Professor in Engineering at UC Berkeley. It is one of the top-ranked textbooks on AI, according to the [Open Syllabus Project](https://analytics.opensyllabus.org/) .

Read [expert recommendations](https://fivebooks.com/book/artificial-intelligence-modern-approach-russell-norvig/)

## [Human Compatible: Artificial Intelligence and the Problem of Control](https://fivebooks.com/book/human-compatible-stuart-russell/)

#### by [Stuart Russell](https://fivebooks.com/people/stuart-russell/)

**\*\*\*🏆 A _Five Books_ Book of the Year \*\*\***

Read [expert recommendations](https://fivebooks.com/book/human-compatible-stuart-russell/)

> “ _Human Compatible_ is a humane book about the challenges and opportunities of AI. It’s also a very non-hype book. By which I mean, it talks about the trajectory of artificial intelligence, the enormous potentials of the pattern recognition on a vast scale that it offers, without indulging fantasies. AI has a great potential to do good and to help us solve problems, but the point is not that it or anybody will ever know best, but rather that we should ensure the values and tendencies encoded into powerful systems are compatible with human thriving, and the thriving of life on this planet. And that compatibility must in turn entail doubt, plurality, and an open interrogation of aims and purposes, not optimisation.” [Read more...](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/tech-ethics-tom-chatfield-technology/)

[The best books on The Ethics of Technology](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/tech-ethics-tom-chatfield-technology/)

[Tom Chatfield](https://fivebooks.com/people/tom-chatfield/) , Journalist

### Interviews where books by Stuart Russell were recommended

### [The best books on The Ethics of Technology , recommended by Tom Chatfield](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/tech-ethics-tom-chatfield-technology/)

We are building ever more powerful machines that will compute answers to any questions we care to ask them, says Tom Chatfield, the author and tech philosopher. But are we asking the right questions? Here, he selects five of the best books on the ethics of technology—thoughtful explorations of how our newly-made tools might remake us.

[The best books on The Ethics of Technology , recommended by Tom Chatfield](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/tech-ethics-tom-chatfield-technology/)

### [Ethics for Artificial Intelligence Books , recommended by Paula Boddington](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/ethics-artificial-intelligence/)

Advances in artificial intelligence pose a myriad of ethical questions, but the most incisive thinking on this subject says more about humans than it does about machines, says Paula Boddington, philosopher and author of a recent [AI ethics textbook](https://fivebooks.com/book/ai-ethics-a-textbook-paula-boddington/) . We first spoke to Paula in 2017—a long time ago in a fast-moving field. This week we caught up with her to find out what’s happened since then and which new books have taken the conversation over ethics and AI further.

[Ethics for Artificial Intelligence Books , recommended by Paula Boddington](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/ethics-artificial-intelligence/)

### [The Best Economics Books of 2019 , recommended by Diane Coyle](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/economics-2019-diane-coyle/)

The urgency of the challenges facing society has led to a wonderful supply of books by leading thinkers on a variety of pressing topics. Economist Diane Coyle, a professor at the University of Cambridge and co-director of the Bennett Institute for Public Policy, recommends her top five economics books of 2019.

[The Best Economics Books of 2019 , recommended by Diane Coyle](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/economics-2019-diane-coyle/)

### [The Best AI Books in 2025 , recommended by ChatGPT](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/the-best-ai-books-in-2025-chatgpt/)

It’s January and time for our annual interview with an AI chatbot. We asked ChatGPT for its latest recommendations for books on artificial intelligence.

[The Best AI Books in 2025 , recommended by ChatGPT](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/the-best-ai-books-in-2025-chatgpt/)

### [The best books on Artificial Intelligence , recommended by ChatGPT](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/artificial-intelligence-gpt-3/)

Normally at _Five Books_ we ask experts to recommend the best books in their field and talk to us about them in an interview, either in person, by phone or via Zoom. In January 2023, we asked the AI bot, ChatGPT, to recommend books to us on the topic of AI. Being an AI doesn’t necessarily make the chatbot an expert on AI books, but we thought it might have some ideas. This week we caught up with ChatGPT to find out if there were any new AI books it wanted to recommend in the year since we spoke.

[The best books on Artificial Intelligence , recommended by ChatGPT](https://fivebooks.com/best-books/artificial-intelligence-gpt-3/)
